Ghouls thrive in places rank with decay and death. A Ghoul haunts a place where it can gorge on dead flesh and decomposing organs. When it can't feed on the dead it pursues living creatures and attempts to make corpses of them. Though they gain no nourishment from corpses they devour Ghouls are driven by an unending hunger that compels them to consume. A Ghoul's undead flesh never rots and this monster can persist in a crypt or tomb for untold ages without feeding.

Ghouls trace their origins to the Abyss. Doresain the first of their kind was an Elf worshiper of the demon lord Orcus. Turning against his own people he feasted on humanoid flesh to honor the Demon Prince of Undeath. As a reward for his service Orcus transformed Doresain into the first Ghoul. Doresain served Orcus faithfully in the Abyss creating Ghouls from the Demon lord's other servants until an incursion by Yeenogh the demonic Gnoll Lord robbed Doresain of his abyssal domain. When Orcus would not intervene on his behalf Doresain turned to the Elf gods for salvation they took pity on him and helped him escape certain destruction. Re-read end of arc 1 a bit.

So from what I understood, ||G-Ramona saved the consciousness of the Keeper, or at least a part of it, while the rest of their "body" fell asleep for eternity||
If I had to liken it to something, I guess I'd be like losing a part of your battery/energy ressource
But ||the consciousness/soul itself is the same||
||The nap doesn't seem to be a long-term solution though||
||Given there's obviously still a connection between the consciousness and the sleeping "body", I wonder if that will play a role in the future||

Found this while searching for stuff about what I wrote above

I think it's more correct to think of ||arc 2 Keeper as an "extension"

They're the exact same Keeper as arc 1, same consciousness, same soul, but it's literally like if you were asleep but one part of you just woke up, while the rest of your body was still resting

They're still connected since they're the same consciousness, so everything the keeper experiences in arc 2 is also "lived" by their leftover body behind the gate

I think it might actually have consequences and issues with the Keeper's goal of returning to the Arc 1 World, but that's just a theory for now||

However, for all intent and purpose, ||when it comes to the continuity of the self, consciousness and soul, they're one and the same, there isn't exactly a "main" or "piece" Keeper

But as a result they lack their power in arc 2||
